explanation: The requirement specifies that 'prop_0' must never occur at any point in time. In LTL, 'G !prop_0' captures this by stating that globally (at all times), 'prop_0' is not true.
The requirement states that 'prop_0' must never occur at any point in time, i.e., 'prop_0' is always false globally.

The requirement states that 'prop_0' should not occur at any point in time. In LTL, this means that globally, 'prop_0' is not true anywhere on any path. This is written using the globally operator 'G' and the negation '!'.
The requirement states that 'prop_0' should never be true during the system's operation. In LTL, this is expressed as 'Globally not prop_0'.
The statement requires that 'prop_0' must never be true at any time. In LTL, this can be stated as 'globally, not prop_0'.
The requirement 'shall not prop_0' means that prop_0 should never be true at any moment during execution, i.e., it is always false globally.
The requirement specifies that 'prop_0' should never be true at any point in time, i.e., globally not prop_0. This is represented using the globally (G) operator and negation.
The requirement says that 'prop_0' must never occur at any point in time, i.e., it is always false globally.
The requirement specifies that prop_0 must never happen at any point in time. In LTL, this is represented by globally NOT prop_0 (G !prop_0).
The requirement says that prop_0 must never occur. In LTL, this is expressed as globally not prop_0.
